item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mythology points: +1
In all seriousness, I could see the logs in the back of my mind. Your use of visual imagery is phenomenal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm strongly reminded of the torment of Prometheus… but also of that other SCP with the blue blooded fellow who keeps showing up with random corpses and burying them. Anyway, +1 for a well written article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this one, but I can't figure out what is happening. Maybe it's the jetlag.

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Criticism
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed this!

There’s an interesting and mysterious (in a good way) conflict between SCP-4859-B and the vultures built up here that we’re given just enough hints on to sort-of work out, with not so many that you’re flat-out stating anything and spoiling the mystery.

+ Tanhony rambles
- TURN IT OFF

A few little things that jumped out at me while reading:

There is no known trigger for this manifestation event, as it occurs at random.

This seems like the Foundation is giving up a little too easily for me. Just because the trigger isn’t known, doesn’t mean that it is actually random. Personally, I’d rephrase this to acknowledge that there could have been a trigger here, we just didn’t find out what it was.

SCP-4859 will manifest. Passersby fail to notice the object.

I could be wrong about this, but I think the second sentence should be ‘Passersby will fail to notice the object here’, just to keep the tense consistent. Same on the next sentence, too.

Soon after, Hay begins laughing. Within eight seconds, all people present in the food court begin laughing except SCP-4859-B. SCP-4859-B stands up, bringing their rifle to bear.

I have to agree with TheMightyMcB here; this is fantastic imagery, and you really get the sense of the kind of conflict SCP-4859-B is involved with just from these three sentences!

Identified to be ectoplasm.

I love the casual mention of ectoplasm here; the idea of these things just being accepted fact in the anomalous world is always really appealing to me. Plus, just having this be a known quantity lets us forgo any long technobabble explanation and just bring in the associations we already have with ectoplasm: spooky shit.

+1

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to Tanhony or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Solid stuff, one nitpick -

There is no known trigger for this manifestation event, as it occurs at random.

That's a bit of a tautology - "it's random because it's random". I'd suggest amending it to something like "No pattern or common element triggering these events have been discovered, and it is considered to be random," or "This event appears to happen at random intervals, as no connection (other than the elements of the event itself) has been found between known manifestations."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh this new ending is just rude. Poor guy cant get it back :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Soon after, Hay begins laughing. Within eight seconds, all people present in the food court begin laughing except SCP-4859-B. SCP-4859-B stands up, bringing their rifle to bear.

God this is such an awesome series of events. Awesome stuff RTME!

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
4 months later…

I've had this idea rattling in my head for a while and I finally wanted to write it. So here we are.

I'll probably make this author post more interesting later. For now, the important parts.

Valued Critters: Mistopheles, gee0765, Crashington, Weryllium, TheMightyMcB, Reverend Fox. Thank you all for your help making this shine.

And thank you for reading.

If you're interested in more of my work, you can find my author page here.
